puzzle.title: The Gift of Divine Insight
puzzle.intro: A servant of Allah was granted a profound gift, not of prophecy, but of deep understanding. Through this divine endowment, this individual offered timeless counsel, a legacy of guidance recorded for all generations. Your task is to uncover the identity of this wise figure and the nature of the wisdom they imparted.
clues: I was granted a special gift from Allah, for which I was commanded to be grateful.
My name is mentioned in the Quran, and an entire chapter is named after me.
I was not a prophet, but I was blessed with hikmah, or wisdom.
I offered advice to my son, urging him not to associate any partners with Allah, calling it a great injustice.
My counsel to my son included lessons on humility, advising him not to turn his cheek in pride from people nor to walk upon the earth with insolence.
I taught my son that even if a matter were the weight of a mustard seed, hidden in a rock or in the heavens or the earth, Allah would bring it forth.

trial.label: What is the core of my story?
options: Building a great barrier against Gog and Magog
Imparting wisdom and guidance to my son
Boasting about my vast treasures and keys
Being swallowed by a giant fish
trial.answer: Imparting wisdom and guidance to my son

location.label: What is the central gift I was given?
options: Control over the winds and jinn

outcome.label: What was the primary sin I warned against?

puzzle.arabic: وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا لُقْمَـٰنَ ٱلْحِكْمَةَ أَنِ ٱشْكُرْ لِلَّهِ ۚ وَمَن يَشْكُرْ فَإِنَّمَا يَشْكُرُ لِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَن كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَنِىٌّ حَمِيدٌۭ
puzzle.verse: And We had certainly given Luqmān wisdom [and said], "Be grateful to Allāh." And whoever is grateful is grateful for [the benefit of] himself. And whoever denies [His favor] - then indeed, Allāh is Free of need and Praiseworthy. (31:12)
